sections.label: Section 1.1
sections.original: ἄριστον μὲν ὕδωρ , ὁ δὲ χρυσὸς αἰθόμενον πῦρ
sections.translation: Water is best , and gold , like a blazing fire in the night , stands out supreme of all lordly wealth . But if , my heart , you wish to sing of contests ,

sections.label: Section 1.5
sections.original: μηκέθʼ ἁλίου σκόπει
sections.translation: look no further for any star warmer than the sun , shining by day through the lonely sky , and let us not proclaim any contest greater than Olympia , From there glorious song enfolds the wisdom of poets , [*] so that they loudly sing

sections.label: Section 1.10
sections.original: Κρόνου παῖδʼ ἐς ἀφνεὰν ἱκομένους
sections.translation: the son of Cronus , when they arrive at the rich and blessed hearth of Hieron ,

sections.label: Section 1.12
sections.original: θεμιστεῖον ὃς ἀμφέπει σκᾶπτον ἐν πολυμάλῳ
sections.translation: who wields the scepter of law in Sicily of many flocks , reaping every excellence at its peak , and is glorified

sections.label: Section 1.15
sections.original: μουσικᾶς ἐν ἀώτῳ ,
sections.translation: by the choicest music , which we men often play around his hospitable table . Come , take the Dorian lyre down from its peg , if the splendor of Pisa and of Pherenicus placed your mind under the influence of sweetest thoughts ,

sections.label: Section 1.20
sections.original: ὅτε παρʼ Ἀλφεῷ σύτο , δέμας
sections.translation: when that horse ran swiftly beside the Alpheus , not needing to be spurred on in the race , and brought victory to his master ,

sections.label: Section 1.23
sections.original: Συρακόσιον ἱπποχάρμαν βασιλῆα . λάμπει δέ οἱ κλέος
sections.translation: the king of Syracuse who delights in horses . His glory shines in the settlement of fine men founded by Lydian Pelops ,

sections.label: Section 1.25
sections.original: τοῦ μεγασθενὴς ἐράσσατο γαιάοχος
sections.translation: with whom the mighty holder of the earth Poseidon fell in love , when Clotho took him out of the pure cauldron , furnished with a gleaming ivory shoulder . Yes , there are many marvels , and yet I suppose the speech of mortals beyond the true account can be deceptive , stories adorned with embroidered lies ;

sections.label: Section 1.30
sections.original: Χάρις δʼ , ἅπερ ἅπαντα τεύχει τὰ μείλιχα θνατοῖς ,
sections.translation: and Grace , who fashions all gentle things for men , confers esteem and often contrives to make believable the unbelievable . But the days to come are the wisest witnesses .

sections.label: Section 2.1
sections.original: ἀναξιφόρμιγγες ὕμνοι ,
sections.translation: Songs , rulers of the lyre , what god , what hero , what man shall we celebrate ? Indeed , Pisa belongs to Zeus ; and Heracles established the Olympic festival , as the finest trophy of battle ;

sections.label: Section 2.5
sections.original: Θήρωνα δὲ τετραορίας ἕνεκα νικαφόρου
sections.translation: and Theron must be proclaimed because of his victorious four-horse chariot , Theron who is just in his regard for guests , and is the bulwark of Acragas , the strength of the city , the choicest bloom of illustrious ancestors ,

sections.label: Section 2.8
sections.original: καμόντες οἳ πολλὰ θυμῷ
sections.translation: who labored much with their spirits , and won a sacred home by the river , and were

sections.label: Section 2.10
sections.original: 20 ὀφθαλμός , αἰὼν δʼ ἔφεπε μόρσιμος , πλοῦτόν τε καὶ χάριν ἄγων
sections.translation: the eye of Sicily ; their allotted lifetime attended them , bringing wealth and grace to their inborn excellence . But you , son of Cronus and Rhea , who rule over your home on Olympus , and over the foremost of festivals , and over the ford of Alpheus , be warmed by our songs and graciously preserve their ancestral land

sections.label: Section 2.15
sections.original: λοιπῷ γένει . τῶν δὲ πεπραγμένων
sections.translation: for their future generations . When deeds have been accomplished , whether justly or contrary to justice , not even Time the father of all things could undo the outcome . But forgetfulness may come , with favorable fortune . Under the power of noble joys , malignant pain

sections.label: Section 2.20
sections.original: παλίγκοτον δαμασθέν ,
sections.translation: is subdued and dies ,

sections.label: Section 2.21
sections.original: ὅταν θεοῦ Μοῖρα πέμπῃ
sections.translation: whenever god-sent Fate lifts prosperity on high . This saying applies to the daughters of Cadmus on their lovely thrones : they suffered greatly , but their heavy sorrow collapsed in the presence of greater blessings .

sections.label: Section 2.25
sections.original: ζώει μὲν ἐν Ὀλυμπίοις ἀποθανοῖσα βρόμῳ
sections.translation: Long-haired Semele , who died in the roar of the thunderbolt , lives among the Olympians ; Pallas is her constant friend , and indeed so is father Zeus , and she is loved by her ivy-crowned son .

sections.label: Section 2.28
sections.original: λέγοντι δʼ ἐν καὶ θαλάσσᾳ
sections.translation: And they say that even in the sea , among the ocean-daughters of Nereus , immortal life

sections.label: Section 2.30
sections.original: Ἰνοῖ τετάχθαι τὸν ὅλον ἀμφὶ χρόνον . ἤτοι βροτῶν γε κέκριται
sections.translation: is granted to Ino for all time . Truly , for mortal men at least , the time when we will reach the limit of death is by no means fixed , nor when we will bring a peaceful day , the sun ’ s child , to an end in unworried well-being . But at various times various currents , both of pleasure and of toil , come to men .

sections.label: Section 3.1
sections.original: Τυνδαρίδαις τε φιλοξείνοις ἁδεῖν καλλιπλοκάμῳ θʼ Ἑλένᾳ
sections.translation: I pray that I may be pleasing to the hospitable sons of Tyndareus and to Helen of the beautiful hair while I honor renowned Acragas by raising my song in praise of Theron ’ s victory at Olympia , won by the choicest of horses with untiring feet . With this in view the Muse stood beside me when I found a shining new manner

sections.label: Section 3.5
sections.original: Δωρίῳ φωνὰν ἐναρμόξαι πεδίλῳ
sections.translation: of fitting the splendid voice of the victory procession to the Dorian sandal .

sections.label: Section 3.6
sections.original: 10 ἀγλαόκωμον . ἐπεὶ χαίταισι μὲν ζευχθέντες ἔπι στέφανοι
sections.translation: For the garlands twined around his hair exact from me this sacred debt , to blend harmoniously for the son of Aenesidamus the embroidered song of the lyre and the cry of the flutes with the arrangement of words , and Pisa bids me to raise my voice — Pisa , from which

sections.label: Section 3.10
sections.original: θεόμοροι νίσοντʼ ἐπʼ ἀνθρώπους ἀοιδαί ,
sections.translation: god-fated songs come often to men ,

sections.label: Section 3.11
sections.original: 20 ᾧ τινι , κραίνων ἐφετμὰς Ἡρακλέος προτέρας ,
sections.translation: for anyone over whose brow the strict Aetolian judge of the Greeks tosses up around his hair the gray-green adornment of olive leaves , fulfilling the ancient behests of Heracles ; the olive which once the son of Amphitryon brought from the shady springs of the Danube ,

sections.label: Section 3.15
sections.original: μνᾶμα τῶν Οὐλυμπίᾳ κάλλιστον ἄθλων
sections.translation: to be the most beautiful memorial of the Olympian contests ,

sections.label: Section 3.16
sections.original: δᾶμον Ὑπερβορέων πείσαις Ἀπόλλωνος θεράποντα λόγῳ .
sections.translation: when he had persuaded the Hyperborean people , the servants of Apollo , with speech . With trustworthy intentions he was entreating them for a shady plant , to be shared by all men and to be a garland of excellence in the grove of Zeus which is hospitable to all . For already the altars had been consecrated to his father , and in mid-month the full

sections.label: Section 3.20
sections.original: ἑσπέρας ὀφθαλμὸν ἀντέφλεξε Μήνα ,
sections.translation: evening ’ s eye shone brightly , the Moon on her golden chariot ,

sections.label: Section 3.21
sections.original: καὶ μεγάλων ἀέθλων ἁγνὰν κρίσιν καὶ πενταετηρίδʼ ἁμᾶ
sections.translation: and he had established the consecrated trial of the great games along with the four years ’ festival beside the sacred banks of the Alpheus . But Pelops ’ sacred ground was not flourishing with beautiful trees in the valleys below the hill of Cronus . He saw that this garden , bare of trees , was exposed to the piercing rays of the sun .

sections.label: Section 3.25
sections.original: δὴ τότʼ ἐς γαῖαν πορεύεν θυμὸς ὥρμα
sections.translation: And so his spirit prompted him to travel to the land

sections.label: Section 3.26
sections.original: Ἰστρίαν νιν · ἔνθα Λατοῦς ἱπποσόα θυγάτηρ
sections.translation: of the Danube , where the horse-driving daughter of Leto had received him when he came from the mountain-glens and deep , winding valleys of Arcadia ; through the commands of Eurystheus , compulsion from his father urged him on the quest of the doe with the golden horns , which once Taÿgete

sections.label: Section 3.30
sections.original: ἀντιθεῖσʼ Ὀρθωσίᾳ ἔγραψεν ἱράν .
sections.translation: had inscribed as a sacred dedication to Artemis who sets things right .

sections.label: Section 4.1
sections.original: ἐλατὴρ ὑπέρτατε βροντᾶς ἀκαμαντόποδος Ζεῦ · τεαὶ γὰρ ὧραι
sections.translation: Charioteer of the thundercloud with untiring feet , highest Zeus ! Your Seasons , whirling to the embroidered notes of the lyre ’ s song , sent me as a witness of the most lofty games . When friends are successful , the noble immediately smile on

sections.label: Section 4.5
sections.original: ποτὶ γλυκεῖαν ἐσλοί .
sections.translation: the sweet announcement . Son of Cronus , you who hold Aetna , the wind-swept weight on terrible hundred-headed Typhon , receive , for the sake of the Graces , this Olympic victory-procession ,

sections.label: Section 4.10
sections.original: χρονιώτατον φάος εὐρυσθενέων ἀρετᾶν . Ψαύμιος γὰρ ἵκει
sections.translation: this most enduring light of widely powerful excellence . For the procession comes in honor of Psaumis ’ chariot ; Psaumis , who , crowned with the olive of Pisa , hurries to rouse glory for Camarina . May the god be gracious to his future prayers , since I praise a man who is most eager in the raising of horses ,

sections.label: Section 4.15
sections.original: χαίροντά τε ξενίαις πανδόκοις
sections.translation: who rejoices in being hospitable to all guests , and whose pure thoughts are turned towards city-loving peace . I will not stain my words with lies . Perseverance is what puts men to the test ,

sections.label: Section 4.19
sections.original: ἅπερ Κλυμένοιο παῖδα
sections.translation: and what saved the son of Clymenus

sections.label: Section 4.20
sections.original: Λαμνιάδων γυναικῶν
sections.translation: from the contempt of the Lemnian women . He won the foot race in bronze armor , and said to Hypsipyle as he went to take the garland : “ Such is my swiftness ;

sections.label: Section 4.25
sections.original: χεῖρες δὲ καὶ ἦτορ ἴσον .
sections.translation: and I have hands and heart to match . Even on young men gray hair often grows , even before the expected age .”

sections.label: Section 5.1
sections.original: ὑψηλᾶν ἀρετᾶν καὶ στεφάνων ἄωτον γλυκὺν
sections.translation: Daughter of Ocean , with a smiling heart receive the sweet bloom of lofty excellence and Olympian garlands , the gifts of Psaumis and of his mule car team with untiring feet .

sections.label: Section 5.4
sections.original: ὃς τὰν σὰν πόλιν αὔξων , Καμάρινα , λαοτρόφον
sections.translation: Psaumis who , exalting your city , Camarina , which cares for its people ,

sections.label: Section 5.5
sections.original: 10 βωμοὺς ἓξ διδύμους ἐγέραιρεν ἑορταῖς θεῶν μεγίσταις
sections.translation: honored the six double altars , at the greatest festivals of the gods , with the sacrifice of oxen and in contests on the fifth day ,

sections.label: Section 5.7
sections.original: ἵπποις ἡμιόνοις τε μοναμπυκίᾳ τε . τὶν δὲ κῦδος ἁβρόν
sections.translation: contests of horse teams , and mule teams , and of riding the single horse . To you he has dedicated rich renown by his victory , and he had his father Acron and his new-founded home proclaimed by the herald .

sections.label: Section 5.9
sections.original: 20 ἵκων δʼ Οἰνομάου καὶ Πέλοπος παρʼ εὐηράτων
sections.translation: Coming from the lovely homes of Oenomaus and of Pelops ,

sections.label: Section 5.10
sections.original: σταθμῶν , ὦ πολιάοχε Παλλάς , ἀείδει μὲν ἄλσος ἁγνὸν
sections.translation: he sings of your sacred grove , Pallas protector of the city , and of the river Oanis , and the local lake ,

sections.label: Section 5.12
sections.original: καὶ σεμνοὺς ὀχετούς , Ἵππαρις οἷσιν ἄρδει στρατόν ,
sections.translation: and the sacred canals with which Hipparis waters its people , and swiftly builds a tall-standing grove of steadfast dwellings , bringing this host of citizens out of helplessness into the light .

sections.label: Section 5.15
sections.original: αἰεὶ δʼ ἀμφʼ ἀρεταῖσι πόνος δαπάνα τε μάρναται πρὸς ἔργον
sections.translation: Always , when it is a question of excellence , toil and expense strive to accomplish a deed that is shrouded in danger ; those who are successful seem wise , even to their fellow-citizens .

sections.label: Section 5.17
sections.original: 40 Σωτὴρ ὑψινεφὲς Ζεῦ , Κρόνιόν τε ναίων λόφον
sections.translation: Savior Zeus , high in the clouds , you who dwell on the hill of Cronus and honor the wide-flowing Alpheus and the sacred cave of Ida ! I come as your suppliant , singing to the sound of Lydian flutes ,

sections.label: Section 5.20
sections.original: αἰτήσων πόλιν εὐανορίαισι τάνδε κλυταῖς
sections.translation: entreating you to adorn this city with glorious hosts of noble men ; and that you , Psaumis the Olympic victor , delighting in the horses of Poseidon , may carry on to the end a pleasurable old age

sections.label: Section 5.23
sections.original: υἱῶν , Ψαῦμι , παρισταμένων . ὑγίεντα δʼ εἴ τις ὄλβον ἄρδει ,
sections.translation: with your sons standing beside you . If a man cultivates both prosperity and health , being generous with his possessions and winning praise as well , let him not seek to become a god .

sections.label: Section 6.1
sections.original: χρυσέας ὑποστάσαντες εὐτειχεῖ προθύρῳ θαλάμου
sections.translation: [*] Raising the fine-walled porch of our dwelling with golden pillars , we will build , as it were , a marvellous hall ; at the beginning of our work we must place a far-shining front . If someone were an Olympic victor ,

sections.label: Section 6.5
sections.original: βωμῷ τε μαντείῳ ταμίας Διὸς ἐν Πίσᾳ ,
sections.translation: and a guardian of the prophetic altar of Zeus at Pisa , and a fellow-founder of renowned Syracuse , what hymn of praise would that man fail to win , by finding fellow-citizens ungrudging in delightful song ?

sections.label: Section 6.8
sections.original: ἴστω γὰρ ἐν τούτῳ πεδίλῳ δαιμόνιον πόδʼ ἔχων
sections.translation: Let the son of Sostratus know that this sandal fits his divinely-blessed foot . But excellence without danger

sections.label: Section 6.10
sections.original: οὔτε παρʼ ἀνδράσιν οὔτʼ ἐν ναυσὶ κοίλαις
sections.translation: is honored neither among men nor in hollow ships . But many people remember , if a fine thing is done with toil . Hagesias , that praise is ready for you , which once Adrastus ’ tongue rightly spoke for the seer Amphiaraus , son of Oicles , when the earth swallowed up him and his shining horses .

sections.label: Section 6.15
sections.original: ἑπτὰ δʼ ἔπειτα πυρᾶν νεκρῶν τελεσθεισᾶν Ταλαϊονίδας
sections.translation: In Thebes , when the seven pyres of corpses had been consumed , the son of Talaus spoke in this way : “ I long for the eye of my army , a man who was good both as a prophet and at fighting with the spear .” And this holds good as well for the man of Syracuse who is master of our victory-procession . Though I am not prone to quarrel , and not overly fond of victory ,

sections.label: Section 6.20
sections.original: καὶ μέγαν ὅρκον ὀμόσσαις τοῦτό γέ οἱ σαφέως
sections.translation: I would even swear a great oath , and on this point at least I will clearly bear witness for him ; and the honey-voiced Muses will give their consent .

sections.label: Section 6.22
sections.original: ὦ Φίντις , ἀλλὰ ζεῦξον ἤδη μοι σθένος ἡμιόνων ,
sections.translation: Phintis , come now and yoke the strength of mules for me , quickly , so that we can drive the chariot along a clear path , and I can at last arrive at the race of these men .

sections.label: Section 6.25
sections.original: καὶ γένος · κεῖναι γὰρ ἐξ ἀλλᾶν ὁδὸν ἁγεμονεῦσαι
sections.translation: For those mules above all others know how to lead the way along this path , since they have won garlands at Olympia , And so it is right to open for them the gates of song ; and I must go today , in good time , to Pitana , beside the ford of Eurotas .

sections.label: Section 6.29
sections.original: ἅ τοι Ποσειδάωνι μιχθεῖσα Κρονίῳ λέγεται
sections.translation: Pitana , who , it is said , lay with Poseidon son of Cronus ,

sections.label: Section 6.30
sections.original: 50 παῖδα ἰόπλοκον Εὐάδναν τεκέμεν .
sections.translation: and bore a child , violet-haired Evadne . But she hid her unwedded pregnancy in the folds of her robe . And in the appointed month she sent servants , and told them to give the baby to be tended by the hero , Aepytus son of Eilatus , who ruled over the Arcadians at Phaesana , and had his allotted home on the Alpheus ,

sections.label: Section 7.1
sections.original: φιάλαν ὡς εἴ τις ἀφνειᾶς ἀπὸ χειρὸς ἑλὼν
sections.translation: As when someone takes a goblet , all golden , the most prized of his possessions , foaming with the dew of the vine from a generous hand , and makes a gift of it to his young son-in-law , welcoming him with a toast from one home to another ,
